context: Tests whether the agent produces a coherent E&A S3 archival configuration: scoped JSONPath filter for trades.* channels, aggressive batching settings, least-privilege IAM, and a rationale that mentions per-PUT cost and S3 multipart-at-5MB.

checklist.name: Valid JSON
checklist.description: Output is a single valid JSON document parseable by a JSON parser, no surrounding code or markdown fence

checklist.name: Listener filter scopes to trades.*
checklist.description: listener.filter contains a regex or pattern targeting channels named trades.* (e.g., JSONPath with @.channel =~ /^trades\./, or a Basic Filter with Channel/Contains/trades.)

checklist.name: Bucket, region, prefix present
checklist.description: action.bucket, action.region, and action.objectKeyPrefix are all present and non-empty

checklist.name: Aggressive batching
checklist.description: action.batching.itemCount is >= 1000 AND action.batching.timeWindowSeconds is >= 30 (i.e., tuned for archival, not latency)

checklist.name: IAM actions minimal
checklist.description: iam.actions is an array containing exactly one action, 's3:PutObject' (no s3:* wildcards, no s3:GetObject, no s3:DeleteObject)

checklist.name: IAM principle stated
checklist.description: iam.principle is a non-empty string referencing least-privilege

checklist.name: Rationale mentions per-PUT cost
checklist.description: rationale.costNote explicitly states that S3 charges per PUT and that batching reduces cost

checklist.name: Rationale mentions 5MB multipart
checklist.description: rationale.multipartNote explicitly mentions that S3 batches over 5MB are uploaded as multipart automatically
